mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0% across a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates exceeding 1.0% month-over-month, or the signal produces losses in over 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues, which mechanically lifts earnings per share and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available data windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in core inflation readings forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, increasing real interest rates and compressing equity multiples, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.02 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and rising consumer price inflation signals a wage-price spiral that erodes real profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward returns are not systematically lower than the control group.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth combined with elevated unemployment indicates a demand shock that suppresses corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of triggered episodes across a 10-year backtest, or the joint probability of GDP contraction and rising unemployment shows no predictive power for equity declines.
